problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Deep brain stimulation of subthalamic nucleus
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Deep. Deep brain stimulation of subthalamic nucleus directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Optogenetic mouse model with 3 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities. In the expert's mind there are few. — Shunryu Suzuki
